_meta.purpose: Curated gene disease-mechanism + inheritance reference. This is REFERENCE DATA (a code constant, like the ACMG criteria table) — NOT a fallback for a failed API query. Sourced from ClinGen gene-disease validity / dosage-sensitivity curations and OMIM. Drives the Step 3 mechanism-compatibility gate (does a loss-of-function mouse knockout model this gene's disease mechanism?) and the Step 4 IMPC zygosity match.
_meta.mechanism: LoF = disease caused by loss of function (knockout is informative). GoF = gain of function / dominant-active (a knockout CANNOT model it — gate should close). both = documented LoF and GoF disease.
_meta.inheritanceMode: Maps to the IMPC zygosity pulled in Step 4: dominant->heterozygote, recessive->homozygote, x-linked->hemizygote.

LDLR.notes: Familial hypercholesterolemia. Loss of LDL-receptor function reduces LDL clearance. Ldlr knockout mice recapitulate hypercholesterolemia/atherosclerosis, so a knockout phenotype is directly mechanism-applicable.

CACNA1C.notes: Timothy syndrome is caused by GAIN of function (impaired voltage-dependent inactivation of Cav1.2, prolonged calcium current). A loss-of-function knockout does NOT model this mechanism, so a dramatic Cacna1c-null phenotype (even embryonic lethality) is not informative for a GoF missense variant — the gate should close.

KCNQ1.notes: Long-QT type 1 (LQT1): loss-of-function / dominant-negative reduction of the slow delayed-rectifier K+ current (IKs). Recessive Jervell-Lange-Nielsen with deafness. Rare gain-of-function variants cause short-QT/atrial fibrillation. Predominant disease mechanism for missense VUS interpretation is LoF, so a knockout is in principle informative.

BRCA1.notes: Hereditary breast/ovarian cancer; loss of tumor-suppressor function.

TP53.notes: Li-Fraumeni. Both loss-of-function and dominant-negative/gain-of-function missense alleles are documented — mechanism is variant-specific, so model-organism applicability must be interpreted with caution.

MLH1.notes: Lynch syndrome; mismatch-repair loss of function.

CFTR.notes: Cystic fibrosis; loss of CFTR chloride-channel function. Recessive — IMPC homozygous data is the relevant zygosity.

FGFR3.notes: Achondroplasia / thanatophoric dysplasia caused by constitutive (gain-of-function) receptor activation. Fgfr3 knockout mice show skeletal OVERGROWTH (opposite of the disease), confirming a knockout cannot model the GoF mechanism — classic gate-closing example.

PTEN.notes: PTEN hamartoma tumor syndrome; loss of phosphatase tumor-suppressor function.

LMNA.notes: Laminopathies (dilated cardiomyopathy, EDMD, lipodystrophy). Both haploinsufficiency and dominant-negative mechanisms documented depending on allele.

PKD1.notes: Autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease; loss of polycystin-1 function (two-hit). Pkd1-null mice develop cystic kidneys.

COL2A1.notes: Type II collagenopathies (skeletal dysplasias). Dominant-negative for most missense (glycine substitutions) and haploinsufficiency for null alleles.

RET.notes: MEN2 (gain-of-function activating missense) vs Hirschsprung (loss-of-function). Mechanism is strongly variant/domain-specific — a knockout models only the LoF (Hirschsprung) side.

SCN5A.notes: LQT3 (gain-of-function, persistent late current) vs Brugada/conduction disease (loss-of-function). Mechanism is variant-specific.
